title: All the Nodes at a distance of K in a Binary Tree

content.problemStatement:
You are given the **root** of a binary tree, a **target node** (`TreeNode* target`), and an integer **k**.
Your task is to **return all node values that are exactly kkk edges away** from the target node.

The nodes can be in any direction — **upward or downward** — and the answer can be returned **in any order**.

Function Declaration

**Function Name**



distanceKdistanceKdistanceK



**Parameters**



rootrootroot: Pointer to the root node of the binary tree (`TreeNode*`).
targettargettarget: Pointer to the target node in the binary tree (`TreeNode*`).
kkk: An integer representing the exact number of edges distance from the target node.



**Return Value**



Returns a vector of integers containing the values of all nodes that are exactly kkk edges away from the target node.
The order of values in the returned vector does **not** matter.
constraints: All node values are unique\textbf{unique} unique
target\text{target}target is guaranteed to be one of the node values
The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input:
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers:
the **number of nodes** in the tree,
The next line contains the level-order traversal of the binary tree, consisting of node values where nullnullnull represents a null/absent child.
For each test case, output on a new line all node values that are **exactly kkk edges away** from the target node.
The values may be printed in **any order**.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
10
         /  \
        5    15
       / \     \
      2   7     18
         / \
        6   8


The nodes that are 2 edges away from node 7 are 2 and 10.

Explanation:
1
  / \
 2   3


Nodes at distance 1 from node 2 are 1 (the parent).
